{"key_event":"Regeneration consolidation and Liberal exclusion","reasoning":"The centralized 1886 constitutional order, Church-state alliance, censorship, emergency policing, and the quick defeat of the 1895 Liberal revolt raised executive and coercive capacity, though weak taxation, monetary strains, Panama difficulties, and Conservative factionalism limited state consolidation. Civil society fell as Liberal parties, newspapers, secular associations, and regional autonomy were constrained, only partly offset by coffee expansion, transport links, and commercially empowered regional actors.","society_change":-0.6,"society_power":5.1,"state_change":0.8,"state_power":5.4}
